name: Feedback TOP
description: The Feedback TOP creates feedback effects by routing a portion of its downstream output back as its own input each frame. It is the foundation of feedback-zoom trails, motion blur simulation, reaction-diffusion systems, and any effect that accumulates visual history over time. When Bypass Feedback is 0 (active), the operator outputs the contents of its Target TOP from the previous frame instead of passing through its input. This allows a chain of filter TOPs (Transform, Level, Blur, GLSL) to be inserted between the Feedback TOP and its target Null TOP, producing an iterative, self-modifying image stream.
summary: Creates iterative visual feedback effects by re-routing a downstream image back as input each frame. Essential for trails, motion blur, and generative accumulation effects.
details: The feedback loop is formed by pointing the Target TOP parameter at a Null TOP that is positioned DOWNSTREAM of the Feedback TOP. Operators placed between the Feedback output and the target Null modulate the feedback buffer each frame: a Transform TOP zooms or rotates, a Level TOP decays brightness, a Blur TOP smooths aliasing. The loop runs entirely on the GPU.
usage:
1. Place a Feedback TOP in your network.
2. Wire your source image (Noise TOP, Movie File In TOP, etc.) into Feedback input 0.
3. Add filter TOPs after Feedback output: Transform -> Level -> Blur.
4. Place a Null TOP at the end of the chain.
5. Set the Feedback TOP Target TOP parameter to the path of that Null TOP.
6. The loop is now active — output accumulates each frame.
tips: Set the Target TOP parameter to a Null TOP that is DOWNSTREAM of the Feedback TOP — targeting an upstream node breaks the loop.
Use pixel format RGBA16Float on the Feedback TOP to prevent precision loss and colour banding during decay.
Lower the Level TOP Brightness to 0.97–0.99 between Feedback and target to create a slow trail decay without full persistence.
Add a Transform TOP (Scale X/Y = 1.005, Rotate = 0.1) between Feedback and target for the classic zoom-rotate spiral.
Use op('feedback1').par.resetpulse.pulse() from a script to clear the buffer instantly to black.
Set Bypass Feedback to 1 via parameter expression or Python to temporarily pass through the source image without feedback.
Inserting a GLSL TOP between Feedback and target allows per-pixel shader manipulation of the feedback buffer each frame.
Use a Composite TOP with Operand=Add to inject new source material into the loop: Feedback output -> Composite input 0, source -> Composite input 1, Composite -> Null target.
Match the Feedback TOP resolution to the filter chain resolution to avoid unexpected rescaling.
Feedback TOP with Transform SOP chained into a Render TOP creates pseudo-3D layered depth effects.
warnings: The Target TOP must be DOWNSTREAM of the Feedback TOP. If you accidentally point it upstream, TouchDesigner will detect the unresolved cycle and cook may fail.
Very high resolution feedback loops are expensive — each frame the full texture must be read back and written. Use 1920x1080 or lower for interactive framerates.
Pixel format mismatch between Feedback and downstream filters can cause unexpected colour shifts. Set all TOPs in the loop to the same pixel format.
Setting Bypass Feedback to 1 discards accumulated buffer — the history is lost. Use a separate Null TOP to sample the accumulated state before bypassing.

parameters.name: Target TOP
parameters.label: Target TOP
parameters.description: Path to the Null TOP (or any TOP) that is downstream of the Feedback TOP. This closes the feedback loop — the output of that target node is fed back as the input to the Feedback TOP on the next frame. Must point to a node AFTER the Feedback TOP in the cook order.
parameters.tooltip: The downstream Null TOP that closes the feedback loop.

parameters.name: Reset
parameters.label: Bypass Feedback
parameters.description: When set to 1 (On), the Feedback TOP acts as a passthrough — it outputs whatever is connected to its input instead of the feedback buffer. When set to 0 (Off, default), feedback is active and the loop runs.
parameters.tooltip: Toggle to disable the feedback loop and pass the input image through.

parameters.name: Reset Pulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Clears the feedback buffer to black in a single frame. Useful for resetting the accumulated state on demand.
parameters.tooltip: Click to clear the feedback buffer immediately.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description: Controls the resolution of the output texture. 'Use Input' matches the input image resolution. Custom enables the Resolution W/H parameters.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel format of the feedback buffer. Use RGBA16Float or RGBA32Float for smooth decay without banding. 8-bit formats can show stepping when brightness decays below 1/255.

pythonExamples.title: Basic feedback loop setup via Python
pythonExamples.description: Programmatically configure a Feedback TOP and its target in a base component.
pythonExamples.code:
# Set the Target TOP parameter
op('feedback1').par.top = 'null1'

# Bypass feedback (passthrough mode)
op('feedback1').par.reset = 1

# Re-enable feedback
op('feedback1').par.reset = 0

# Pulse reset to clear buffer
op('feedback1').par.resetpulse.pulse()

pythonExamples.title: Animate feedback decay with audio level
pythonExamples.description: Drive Level TOP brightness from an audio amplitude CHOP to make feedback trails pulse with music.
pythonExamples.code:
# In an Execute DAT - onFrameStart
def onFrameStart(frame):
    # Get audio amplitude from Null CHOP reference
    amp = op('null_audio')['chan1']
    # Base decay 0.95, pulse to 0.99 on loud hits
    decay = 0.95 + amp * 0.04
    op('level1').par.brightness = decay

pythonExamples.title: Toggle feedback bypass on keypress
pythonExamples.description: Use a Keyboard In DAT callback to toggle the feedback loop on/off.

pythonExamples.title: Read pixel values from feedback buffer
pythonExamples.description: Sample a specific pixel from the feedback output texture each frame.
pythonExamples.code:
# In a Script DAT or Execute DAT
def onFrameStart(frame):
    top = op('feedback1')
    # numpyArray requires Script TOP or CUDA access;
    # for simple sampling use a CHOP to TOP + pixel lookup
    print(f'Feedback resolution: {top.width}x{top.height}')
    print(f'Pixel format: {top.pixelFormat}')

commonInputs.reason: Generative seed texture for the feedback loop
Video as the seed input that gets accumulated
Blended multi-source input for the loop

commonOutputs.reason: Decay brightness each frame to create trails
Soften accumulated buffer to prevent aliasing
Feedback target node that closes the loop
Per-pixel shader processing of the buffer
